However, it is their mission to get you back into the job market, so if you come well-prepared for your appointment, the odds are very much in your favor. 4. The Agentur für Arbeit or Jobcenter requires beneficiaries to be registered unemployed or facing imminent unemployment. Courses must be attended on a full-time basis to ensure comprehensive training. Hier finden Sie Informationen zu Berufssprachkursen und Sprachförderung der Bundesagentur für Arbeit, die Integration und berufliche Chancen fördern. Learning German Moving to a foreign country means facing a number of challenges, such as mastering a new language and getting used to customs different to one’s own culture. Learning German ensures your successful integration: secure a job in Germany, explore your city, make new friends. First things first, though: find more information on where to learn German below. I am registered with Agentur fur Arbeit but they rejected my requesting for German language course A1 and told me to contact BAMF. Not sure whether I need Berechtigungsschein (eligibility certificate) from AFA or BAMF. Deutsch lernen ist notwendig, um in Deutschland zu arbeiten. Informieren Sie sich hier über Deutschkurse und ob sie finanziert werden. I believe they mentioned something about a voucher I have to request and I have also been reading that I would have to enroll in an integration 'BAMF' certified course, but I find the information a bit confusing. My question is, what is the process of enrolling in a German course through the Agentur für Arbeit (what are the steps)? A Bildungsgutschein is a training voucher issued by the German Agentur für Arbeit (AfA) or Jobcenter. It covers the cost of vocational training courses aimed at helping individuals re-enter the workforce. The voucher includes: Tuition fees for approved courses. Additional costs like travel expenses, accommodation, and childcare (if applicable). If you are not currently in work, simply speak to your intermediary at your local employment agency (Agentur für Arbeit) or job centre (Jobcenter), who will advise which language schools offer vocational language courses. The Bundesagentur für Arbeit, Germany's Federal Employment Agency, is in charge of this online portal, which is intended to assist people in locating and applying to a variety of educational and training opportunities around the nation. Data Analytics Program Fully funded by the German government Develop the skills of a Data analyst, learn how to leverage AI, and work with expert mentors to prepare for a lasting career in tech. All in an online setup, with career coaching to help you land your new job, and 100% of the cost covered by the Agentur für Arbeit. Deutschtest für Zuwanderer (DTZ) The Deutschtest für Zuwanderer (German Language Test for Immigrants - DTZ) is a certification designed specifically for immigrants in Germany and is usually taken at the conclusion of an integration course. Passing this test is equivalent to achieving a CEFR level A2 or B1. The German Arbeitsagentur, commonly referred to as the Jobcenter in English, serves as a pivotal institution for job seekers and employees alike in Germany. Established under the Federal Employment Agency (Bundesagentur für Arbeit), the Jobcentre plays a crucial role in the country’s labor market, overseeing a wide range of services from job placement to unemployment benefits. How to study for free with the Bildungsgutschein You could be eligible to take our courses for free Code Academy Berlin is a certified education institute and can therefore accept the Bildungsgutschein (education voucher) from the Agentur für Arbeit / Jobcenter (Employment agency). UX Design Program Fully funded by the German government Develop the skills of a UX designer, learn how to leverage AI, and work with expert mentors to prepare for a lasting career in tech. All in an online setup, with career coaching to help you land your new job, and 100% of the cost covered by the Agentur für Arbeit. Education in Germany Germany is in need of skilled workers and well-trained staff. A vocational training programme or a course of higher education will pave the way for you to begin a career. Furthermore, it will improve your earning potential and protect you from potential unemployment. Here, we would like to give you an overview of these topics. A Bildungsgutschein is a voucher issued by the German Agentur für Arbeit (the job center) which allows you to study for free. Funded by the German government, it enables you to learn new skills which will help you get back into the job market if you’re unemployed, or to obtain qualifications that are crucial to your current line of work. The Bildungsgutschein is provided by the German job center, called the German Agentur für Arbeit, and in addition to paying for education, other expenses such as travel, accomodation, fees, learning materials, and even childcare may be covered as well. Who is Bildungsgutschein for? 1. Business English - English for the job Our "Fit for the Job" English course, which is funded by the German Federal Employment Agency (Agentur für Arbeit) or the Job Center (Jobcenter), takes place as individual lessons - either as face-to-face lessons at the GLS campus in Berlin Prenzlauer Berg or online. Speaking with a person from the Agentur für Arbeit I was told I could do some German classes, and they gave me the contact of a school in Berlin.
